extraction.one_line_summary: ALAPHILIPPE Julian won from 21-man breakaway after resisting MAJKA's attacks on Xorret de Catí, while FROOME attacked mid-climb to distance GC rivals except CONTADOR.
tactical_patterns: Bora-Hansgrohe launched MAJKA on steepest sections up to 20%, but tactical patience from ALAPHILIPPE neutralized team support advantage.
FROOME attacked at mid-ascent rather than steep finale, creating selection but allowing CONTADOR to rejoin on descent.
OLIVEIRA Nelson virtually took red jersey within breakaway, showing teams willing to surrender GC for intermediate stage control.

rider_signals.ALAPHILIPPE Julian: positive: Won Grand Tour stage debut at 25, showing superior sprint after tiring out domestique-supported MAJKA through patient tactics.
rider_signals.FROOME Chris: positive: Extended GC lead to 28 seconds by mid-climb attack, but inability to drop CONTADOR on descent reveals descending vulnerability.
rider_signals.CONTADOR Alberto: positive: Matched FROOME despite difficult season start and knee injury forcing Tour absence, caught leader on final 2km descent.
rider_signals.MAJKA Rafal: negative: Failed to convert team support and tactical launch on 20% gradients into stage win against solo rider.
